target.name: Atana Elements
target.founder: Tom Wilson (CEO, Stanford GSB + Royal School of Mines, ex-Hess Corp, was early hire and operations/resource lead at Lilac Solutions from 2019)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $28M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Atana Elements is a Seed-stage Climate & Energy company founded by Tom Wilson (ex-Hess Corp, early operations lead at Lilac Solutions) that has raised $27.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers in climate tech alongside 1 public comparable (Lonza Group, LNZA), though narrow peer matching by stage and valuation tier yielded insufficient datapoints for reliable median multiples.

Only one valuation method proved applicable: Stage-Progression analysis, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A advancement. Using $27.5M in total raised as a proxy for current valuation, this method yields $69M. A Peer-Median approach was not pursued because the broader Climate & Energy peer set (Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and others) lacked sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers—a minimum of three datapoints is required for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $58M to $79M, centered on $69M. This range reflects ±15% variance around the Stage-Progression result, accounting for the single-method constraint. Key uncertainties include the absence of transaction-level comparables at Atana's stage and the heterogeneity of climate-tech business models, which limits confidence in stage-based step-ups. Founder pedigree (Stanford GSB, mining geology expertise, Lilac tenure) is noted but not quantified in this valuation.
